sections.voice_notes: Verbatim x_factor + prose_snippet both exceed slot caps. Surfacing both as deferred; outline must return a template with larger heading/tagline caps or mark these non-verbatim. Body summarizes 'about'; buttons use Explore Faith + plan_visit.

copy.body: Welcome to Paradox, an open and affirming church in Redlands where you can be part of the conversation about what it means to trust the love of God in our modern world. We take scripture seriously and questions just as seriously.

sections.voice_notes: Specific invitation, not vague. Body weaves Saturday 10:00 AM at 51 W Olive with the 'hour thinking about something that matters' framing. Item heading = geographic focus; item body = chapel/courtyard detail.

copy.body: Come spend an hour thinking about something that actually matters. Our main worship service is Saturdays at 10:00 AM, 51 W Olive, Redlands, and it runs about 75 minutes. Modern music, a sermon built to start a conversation, and room to sit with the questions you bring in the door.

items.item_heading: Rooted in Redlands and the Inland Empire
items.item_body: You will find us in the smaller building on the right of the First Baptist courtyard, with the service held in the Chapel. The University of Redlands shapes the intellectual culture here, and so do we.

sections.voice_notes: Verbatim value-prop atom 9cdda142 placed in body. REPAIR: verbatim x_factor atom 96869154 now embedded verbatim inside items[0].item_body (it was previously compressed and dropped). Heading compressed from longer x_factor; persona atoms compressed into item headings/bodies, no claims lost.

copy.primary_heading: Built to hold the tradition and the questions
copy.body: Value Proposition: At Paradox, you do not have to choose between your intellect and your faith, and you do not have to have your doubts resolved before you belong. We are an open and affirming community in Redlands that takes scripture seriously, asks hard questions honestly, and measures success not by what you believe but by how you love.

items.item_heading: For people the church stopped making room for
items.item_body: What makes us genuinely different is not that we are progressive, and it is not that we are traditional. It is that we refuse to choose. Most communities that hold progressive values quietly set the tradition aside to get there. And most communities that hold the tradition treat progressive questions as a threat. We hold both, not as a tension to manage but as a paradox to inhabit.

We do well with families where one partner is Christian and the other is atheist or agnostic, because we talk a lot more about how we live than about what we correctly believe. Bring your husband, bring your kids, bring the questions you have carried for a decade.

items.item_heading: For the skeptic who walked in twice
items.item_body: If you came once because someone asked, and again because something surprised you: that is enough. You do not have to trust the institution yet. You can engage the big questions here without being recruited.

sections.voice_notes: Verbatim heading + verbatim ethos line both placed exactly. Body summarizes sermons topic with the live Matthew series; accent_body weaves blog continuation and Dear Maya letter-series format.

copy.primary_heading: Start Discussions, Rather Than End Them
copy.body: Sermons at Paradox are designed to start discussions, rather than end them. You don't have to agree with the pastor to be part of Paradox. The current series walks through Matthew, with recent talks on the Sheep and the Goats and the Parable of the Vineyard. Sample one before you ever set foot in the room.
copy.accent_body: The conversation keeps going in Pastor Craig's weekly blog, on ethics, queer affirmation, racial justice, and more. Each Christmas it turns personal in the Dear Maya, Dear Bode, and Dear Christmas letters.

sections.voice_notes: A pathway, not a funnel. Heading from next_steps; body from connect_groups (discussion groups). Three ministry facts become card headings; kids topic feeds first card body.

copy.body: Sermons at Paradox start conversations, not end them. Our discussion groups give you space to process, ask questions, and grow in community. Most people start in Pastor Craig's group, in person or online. Pick the move that fits where you actually are.

items.item_body: Saturdays at 9:15 AM, your littlest ones learn they are wonderfully made through stories, songs, and play, with a Parents Room when they need to move around. Stick around for the 10:00 service.
Saturdays at 9:15 AM, kids explore the same scriptures taught upstairs, then serve as liturgists, sing with the band, and help with communion. This church is for them, and they know it.
Saturdays at 9:15 AM, young people gather to discuss life, play games, and laugh together before the main service.

sections.voice_notes: REPAIR: verbatim vision atom 154 chars trimmed? No — atom is verbatim, kept EXACTLY; the 404>400 overflow was caused by my appended trailing space/period. Removed the stray trailing space so the verbatim body lands at 400 or under. Body is the verbatim atom alone. Item headings name actual partners and rhythms.

copy.body: We are building toward a Redlands where the people who have been told church is not for them find out that's just not true. Not because we softened anything, but because we made room for their questions, their doubts, and their whole complicated lives. The picture of success for us is not a bigger room on Saturday. It is a city where more people are becoming more loving, and some of that started here.

items.item_body: We walk it alongside the Redlands Family Service Association to feed neighbors who are going without.

items.item_heading: Thanksgiving Food Drive, every November since 2016
items.item_body: A yearly rhythm with Redlands Family Service, the same partner we have shown up for since the beginning.

sections.voice_notes: Low-friction single action for a Saturday, plus a way to tell us she's coming. Heading/body/button from plan_visit; second button url is the say-hello form fact.

copy.body: We are so glad you found us. The common tie between everyone here is a desire to become more loving people. If you share that, come spend a Saturday with us at 10:00 AM, and you can let us know you're coming so we can look for you.

sections.voice_notes: REPAIR: verbatim prose-snippet atom 9f2abd91 is 121 chars and exceeds the 100-char primary_heading cap. Surfaced as deferred_slot; substituted a short LABEL heading so the section still has a valid heading. Body compresses the 2016 origin story, every claim kept. Button uses branded term.

copy.body: In 2016, a small group of friends started Paradox with arms wide open to inclusion, minds wide open to intellect, and hearts wide open to compassion. In 2018, we became the second open and affirming church in Redlands. We are still figuring this out, and we would love to figure it out alongside you.
